# Missing aiConfig.engines.neo causes SessionSummarization test failures

## Context

Surfaced during #10001 ticket-intake (PR #10121, merged 2026-04-20) while running the full `test-unit memory-core/` baseline. Five Playwright specs under `SessionSummarization.spec.mjs` fail identically with `TypeError: Cannot set properties of undefined (setting 'dataDir')` at the test-setup line `aiConfig.engines.neo.dataDir = tmpDir`. The test expects `aiConfig.engines.neo` to be defined; the current config shipped in both `config.template.mjs` and the gitignored `config.mjs` mirror exposes only `engines.chroma` (plus `engines.kb.chroma` after #10001's nested-reference addition).

The same 5 failures reproduce on `git stash` baseline, confirming this is pre-existing and unrelated to the caller PR.

## The Problem

The tests reference an `engines.neo` config block that once existed (introduced in #9727 "Semantic Refactoring of Memory Core Configuration", CLOSED) to hold routing for the Neo native SQLite backend. That backend's routing was later consolidated (current SQLite path lives in `storagePaths.graph`), and the `engines.neo` config key was removed. The tests were never updated.

**Reproducer:**

```bash
npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/SessionSummarization.spec.mjs
```

**Observed:** 5 failures at `SessionSummarization.spec.mjs:53:37` with `TypeError: Cannot set properties of undefined (setting 'dataDir')`.

## The Architectural Reality

Two options with asymmetric trade-offs:

**Option A: Restore the `engines.neo` config block.** Adds `engines.neo: {dataDir, filename}` to the config template + gitignored mirror, pointing at a SQLite path analogous to the existing `storagePaths.graph` entry. Tests pass without modification. Downside: resurrects a config block whose downstream consumer was consolidated — operators see an unused key.

**Option B: Update the tests to match the current config shape.** The tests' intent is to point the SQLite backend at a per-test temp-dir path for isolation. Replace `aiConfig.engines.neo.dataDir = tmpDir` + `aiConfig.engines.neo.filename = testDbName` with a mutation of the current routing key (likely `aiConfig.storagePaths.graph = path.join(tmpDir, testDbName)`). Downside: requires tracing the consolidation to confirm the current routing key.

Recommended: **Option B**. Tests should match the real config. Option A re-introduces dead schema surface that will decay again.

## The Fix

1. Locate the consumer(s) of the SQLite backend that `engines.neo` used to feed. Grep for `storagePaths.graph` to confirm current routing.
2. Update `SessionSummarization.spec.mjs` lines 53–54 (and any analogous test-setup elsewhere) to mutate the current routing key instead of `aiConfig.engines.neo.*`.
3. Run the 5 failing tests in isolation; confirm all pass.
4. Run full `test-unit memory-core/` to confirm no cross-spec regression.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `SessionSummarization.spec.mjs` runs 5/5 pass in isolation.
- [ ] Full `test-unit memory-core/` baseline drops from 7 failures to ≤2 (the remaining — `McpServerToolLimits` 1161-char description + `FileSystemIngestor` path-pattern — are pre-existing and out of scope here).
- [ ] No new `engines.neo` config surface introduced. If Option A is chosen despite the recommendation, rationale documented in the commit message.

## Out of Scope

- The `McpServerToolLimits` 1161-char tool description overage (separate ticket).
- The `FileSystemIngestor` path-pattern test failure (separate ticket).
- Broader `SessionSummarization` refactoring beyond the config-routing update.

## Related

- Surfaced in PR #10121 (#10001) intake sweep
- Historical: #9727 "Semantic Refactoring of Memory Core Configuration" (CLOSED) — introduced the `engines.neo` schema that was later consolidated
- Failing spec: `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/SessionSummarization.spec.mjs:53–54`
